dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3665 IS: Passenger Rail Crew Protection Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Duckworth (for herself and Mr. Hoeven) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To prosecute, as a Federal crime, the assault or intimidation of a passenger train crew member in a manner consistent with the prosecution of assault or intimidation of an aircraft crew member.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Passenger Rail Crew Protection Act.

section.header: Interference with passenger train crew members

subsection.text: Chapter 281 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Interference with passenger train crew members

paragraph.text: The term crew member means any of the following employees:

subparagraph.text: An employee performing, or responsible for, a safety-sensitive function.
An employee performing, or responsible for, any operation, function, or customer service at an intercity rail station or commuter rail station.
The term dangerous weapon means a weapon, device, instrument, material, or animate or inanimate substance that is used for, or is readily capable of, causing death or serious bodily injury.

clause.text: a pocket knife with a blade that is less than 21/2 inches in length; and

paragraph.text: The term passenger train means a passenger train in intercity rail passenger transportation (as defined in section 24102) or commuter rail passenger transportation (as defined in section 24102).
The term serious bodily injury means a bodily injury that involves—

subparagraph.text: protracted loss or impairment of the function of—

subsection.text: It shall be unlawful for any person onboard a passenger train in operation, on a platform serving a passenger train in operation, or in a rail station—

paragraph.text: to assault or intimidate a crew member and thereby interfere with the performance of the duties of a crew member or lessen the ability of a crew member to perform those duties; or
to attempt or conspire to perform an act described in paragraph (1).
shall be fined under title 18 or imprisoned for not more than 8 years, or both; and
if a dangerous weapon is used in assaulting or intimidating the crew member, shall be imprisoned for not more than 20 years.

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 281 of title 49, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: § 28104. Interference with passenger train crew members.
